title: Behavior recognition in an enclosure
abstract: An imaging system in an enclosure can capture a plurality of successive images of a user to determine an external behavior (e.g., a gait) of the user. The user can be identified based on the external behavior, and an environment may be controlled according to preferences and/or requests of the user.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for external behavior recognition for control of an environment of a facility, the method comprising: capturing, with an imaging system of the facility, a plurality of successive images of a user of the facility; obtaining, from the plurality of successive images, external behavior data of the user, wherein the external behavior data is representative of one or more physical actions taken by the user during the capturing of the plurality of successive images; determining an identity of the user based at least in part on the external behavior data of the user; and implementing environment customizations associated with the identity of the user. 2. The method of claim 1 , wherein the one or more physical actions taken by the user comprises a gait of the user. 3. The method of claim 1 , wherein the imaging system comprises a camera, an infrared (IR) camera, a lidar sensor, or an imaging radar system. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the imaging system comprises the camera or the IR camera, and wherein the plurality of successive images comprise video frames. 5. The method of claim 1 , wherein the imaging system is operatively coupled with a controller. 6. The method of claim 1 , wherein obtaining the external behavior data of the user comprises extracting a respective pose of the user from each image of the plurality of successive images. 7. The method of claim 1 , wherein determining the identity of the user based at least in part on the external behavior data of the user comprises determining a unique identifier associated with the user. 8. The method of claim 1 , wherein implementing environment customizations comprises controlling an environmental aspect using one or more building systems. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein the one or more building systems comprises a device ensemble having a housing that encloses the one or more devices that comprise: (i) sensors, (ii) a transceiver, or (iii) a sensor and an emitter. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein the device ensemble is disposed in a fixture of the facility, or is attached to a fixture of the facility. 11. The method of claim 9, wherein the one or more building systems comprise a tintable window. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein the tintable window comprises an electrochromic window. 13. The method of claim 9, wherein the device ensemble comprises the imaging system. 14. The method of claim 1 , wherein implementing the environment customizations comprises adjusting a temperature, window tint, and/or lighting within the facility. 15. The method of claim 1 , wherein capturing the plurality of successive images of the user is responsive to a triggering event. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein the triggering event comprises detection of the user at a location of the facility. 17. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the identity of the user is further based on sensor information regarding the user. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the sensor information comprises information indicative of a sound made by the user, dimensions of the user, and/or biometric information regarding the user. 19. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the identity of the user is further based on one or more device inputs received from the user. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein the one or more device inputs comprise a temperature setting, a window tint setting, and/or a lighting setting. 21. The method of claim 20, wherein the lighting setting comprises a brightness and/or color of light. 22. The method of claim 1 , further comprising generating the identity of the user, the identity comprising a unique code or number associated with the user. 23. The method of claim 1, wherein determining the identity of the user comprises using machine learning to determine the identity. 24. The method of claim 23, further comprising training a machine learning model used to perform the machine learning. 25. The method of claim 24, wherein training the machine learning model comprises using previously-obtained sets of images of the user taking the one or more physical actions as a positive dataset. 26. The method of claim 25, wherein the previously-obtained sets of images are obtained by the imaging system. 27. An apparatus for external behavior recognition for control of an environment of a facility, the apparatus comprising one or more controllers comprising circuitry, which one or more controllers are configured to: capture, or direct capturing of, a plurality of successive images of a user of the facility, wherein the plurality of successive images are captured with an imaging system of the facility; obtain, or direct obtaining of, external behavior data of the user from the plurality of successive images, wherein the external behavior data is representative of one or more physical actions taken by the user during the capturing of the plurality of successive images; determine, or direct determining of, an identity of the user based at least in part on the external behavior data of the user; and implement, or direct implementing of, environment customizations associated with the identity of the user. 28. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the one or more physical actions taken by the user comprises a gait of the user. 29. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the imaging system comprises a camera, an infrared (IR) camera, a lidar sensor, or an imaging radar system. 30. The apparatus of claim 29, wherein the imaging system comprises the camera or the IR camera, and wherein the plurality of successive images comprise video frames. 31. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein, to obtain or direct the obtaining of external behavior data of the user, the one or more controllers are configured to extract, or direct extracting of, a respective pose of the user from each image of the plurality of successive images. 32. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein, to determine or direct the determining of the identity of the user based at least in part on the external behavior data of the user, the one or more controllers are configured to determine, or direct determining of, a unique identifier associated with the user. 33. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein, to implement or direct the implementing of environment customizations, the one or more controllers are configured to control, or direct controlling of, an environmental aspect using one or more building systems. 34. The apparatus of claim 33, wherein the one or more building systems comprises a device ensemble having a housing that encloses the one or more devices that comprise: (i) sensors, (ii) a transceiver, or (iii) a sensor and an emitter. 35. The apparatus of claim 34, wherein the device ensemble is disposed in a fixture of the facility, or is attached to a fixture of the facility. 36. The apparatus of claim 34, wherein the one or more building systems comprise a tintable window. 37. The apparatus of claim 36, wherein the tintable window comprises an electrochromic window. 38. The apparatus of claim 34, wherein the device ensemble comprises the imaging system. 39. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein, to implement or direct the implementing of environment customizations, the one or more controllers are configured to adjust, or direct adjusting of, a temperature, window tint, and/or lighting within the facility. 40. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the one or more controllers are configured to capture or direct the capturing of the plurality of successive images of the user responsive to a triggering event. 41. The apparatus of claim 40, wherein the triggering event comprises detection of the user at a location of the facility. 42. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the one or more controllers are configured to determine or direct the determining of the identity of the user further based on sensor information regarding the user. 43. The apparatus of claim 42, wherein the sensor information comprises information indicative of a sound made by the user, dimensions of the user, and/or biometric information regarding the user. 44. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the one or more controllers are configured to determine or direct the determining of the identity of the user further based on one or more device inputs received from the user. 45. The apparatus of claim 44, wherein the one or more device inputs comprise a temperature setting, a window tint setting, and/or a lighting setting. 46. The apparatus of claim 45, wherein the lighting setting comprises a brightness and/or color of light. 47. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein the one or more controllers are further configured to generate, or direct the generating of, the identity of the user, the identity comprising a unique code or number associated with the user. 48. The apparatus of claim 27, wherein, to determine or direct the determining of the identity of the user the one or more controllers are configured to use, or direct the using of, machine learning to determine the identity. 49. The apparatus of claim 48, wherein the one or more controllers are further configured to train, or direct training of, a machine learning model used to perform the machine learning. 50. The apparatus of claim 49, wherein, to train or direct the training of the machine learning model, the one or more controllers are configured to use, or direct the using of, previously- 

obtained sets of images of the user taking the one or more physical actions as a positive dataset. 51. The apparatus of claim 50, wherein the previously-obtained sets of images are obtained by the imaging system. 52. A non-transitory computer program product for external behavior recognition for control of an environment of a facility, which non-transitory computer program product contains instructions inscribed thereon that, when executed by one or more processors, cause the one or more processors to execute operations comprising: capturing, with an imaging system of the facility, a plurality of successive images of a user of the facility; obtaining, from the plurality of successive images, external behavior data of the user, wherein the external behavior data is representative of one or more physical actions taken by the user during the capturing of the plurality of successive images; determining an identity of the user based at least in part on the external behavior data of the user; and implementing environment customizations associated with the identity of the user. 53.
